intro: Apple is raising prices on iPads and MacBooks, citing increased chip costs amid demand for AI components, with potential iPhone price hikes to follow. OpenAI released its GPT-5.6 model to vetted partners after the Trump administration requested a staged rollout over safety concerns. OpenAI is acquiring Jony Ive's hardware company io and may delay its planned 2026 IPO to 2027.

whitelist.title: Apple raises iPad and MacBook prices, blaming cost of chips amid AI boom
whitelist.desc: Company says it cannot shield customers from memory and storage chip costs – and iPhone hikes could be next

whitelist.title: Apple’s Vision Pro and smart glasses leader reportedly leaves for OpenAI.
whitelist.desc: With its Vision Pro headset suddenly even more expensive, and plans for smart glasses apparently in the works, Bloomberg reports Apple is losing Paul Meade, the hardware VP who was in charge of those projects. The report says he’ll reunite with former Apple designers, including Jony Ive, Tang Tan, and Evans Hankey, at OpenAI, which purchased their startup last year, as the executive shakeup under new Apple CEO John Ternus continues. [Link: Apple’s Vision Pro and Smart Glasses Chief to Join OpenA...

whitelist.title: OpenAI unveils GPT-5.6 amid US AI regulatory drama
